title: Punish the Wandering Queen
setup: Black has lashed out with an early **Qh4**, hoping for cheap threats. White's pawns are about to make her pay.
hint: When the enemy queen pokes out early, kick her with a humble pawn and gain a free tempo.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a **trapping / tempo-gaining** motif. Black's premature {{Qh4}} placed the queen on an active-looking square, but with no support — and right next to a pawn that is about to promote her into a fugitive.

White plays {{g3}}, attacking the queen with a pawn.

## Why Black Suffers

- The queen has very few good retreat squares. {{Qh3}} or {{Qh5}} keeps her active but White has gained a free move of development.
- {{Qxe4}}? Not possible — there's nothing to grab there.
- {{Qxh2}} runs into {{Rxh2}}, losing the queen for a rook.

Every move the queen wastes is a tempo White uses to develop knights, bishops, and castle.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Don't bring your queen out early — she's the most valuable piece, so every attacker is a free tempo for the opponent.

When *they* break this rule, **chase the queen with cheap pieces**: pawns and minor pieces gain time while the queen scurries home.
